behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with real git rename detection and stdout parsing. A common real bug is invoking git without rename detection flags, which yields delete+add and cannot be caught by pure in-process fixtures. Behavior text is tied to repository analysis output, so real subprocess + filesystem repo state is the minimum. Higher levels are disqualified because the behavior includes no AI/ML decision or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo, and assert stdout report has the file only under renamed and not under added/deleted.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This failure often appears only with real git diff behavior on binary content (encoding/format/exit behavior) and real file bytes. Mocked diff text can miss decode/parsing crashes triggered by actual subprocess output. No AI/ML output dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo with a committed binary file change, invoke the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, no crash, and binary file is reflected in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure mode is real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (specific non-zero exit and stderr semantics). In-process stubs can simulate this, but they can also hide command/exit-path mismatches in the actual CLI subprocess handling. No model output quality or multi-agent steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert successful handling with an empty structured report and no thrown error path.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real commits, file operations, and git subprocess output. Integration with mocked git responses can validate counting logic but cannot catch mismatches between command selection, git semantics, and parsing over real history. No AI/ML dependency; no agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with known sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert reported category counts exactly match the known history ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/filesystem and subprocess-observed behaviors. Fakes can miss real discrepancies in how the CLI invokes git for staged/unstaged states. Behavior text does not involve AI/ML decisions, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run the CLI subprocess for each mode, and assert structured stdout outputs are identical.
